column1: # mapping_set_title: Open Policy Agent (OPA) to W3C Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV) Mappings
# mapping_set_description: Schema-level mappings between Open Policy Agent (OPA)
#   LinkML classes and the W3C Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV). OPA is widely
#   deployed for privacy and consent decisions on personal data, and DPV
#   already provides the canonical vocabulary for policies, authorisation
#   procedures, access control methods, decision-making, and enforcement of
#   access control. Predicates - skos:closeMatch where the two classes name
#   the same conceptual artefact (most notably openpolicyagent:Policy and
#   dpv:Policy, both denoting a normative ruleset over data processing);
#   skos:relatedMatch where the OPA class realises only part of a DPV concept
#   (e.g. an AuthorizationInput as a specific Access processing event);
#   skos:broadMatch where the OPA class wraps multiple DPV concerns (e.g. a

column10: An OPA IR Policy is a normative ruleset governing what is or is not permitted on data; semantically and lexically aligned with dpv:Policy
When a Rego policy decides access to personal data it functions as a concrete realisation of a DPV AccessControlMethod
An AuthorizationInput is the documented input to an authorization procedure - aligned with the DPV AuthorisationProcedure technical/organisational measure
Each authorization decision corresponds to a dpv:Access processing event over the targeted resource
A WasmResolver exposes a policy-decision entrypoint that operationally enforces access control over the runtime data
A bundle Manifest is a delivery container for one or many dpv:Policy artefacts; broader than any single policy
A built-in function invoked during policy evaluation contributes to the decision-making processing operation
